ideation: This animation sequence will simulate the act of shuffling, using both arms to mimic the motion of mixing or rearranging items.  The animation will focus on a fluid, alternating movement of the arms, suggesting a sense of playful randomness.  We'll use a three-frame sequence for simplicity and impact. In the first frame, both arms will start in a neutral position at the sides, ready to begin the shuffling action. The second frame will show a dynamic contrast; the right arm moves upward and outward, while the left arm moves downward and inward, suggesting the separation and rearrangement of items.  The third frame reverses this motion, with the left arm now moving upward and outward, and the right arm moving downward and inward, completing one cycle of the shuffle. This alternating pattern implies the continuous random mixing associated with shuffling. The wrists will remain mostly neutral throughout, focusing on the larger arm movements for clarity.

frames.beat: arms rest at sides, ready to shuffle
right arm lifts as left arm dips, mixing begins
left arm lifts as right arm dips, continuing the shuffle
